location.office_time_required: minimum of 2 days in office per week, with most teams in office between 2-3 days a week

level_signals: Engages directly with C-level and senior leadership
'Significant experience as a product leader at a large-scale B2C company'
Expected to 'anchor' the loyalty program and partner across the tribe

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Significant experience as a product leader at a large-scale B2C company — you've built consumer products at scale and have strong intuition for customer behavior and emotional drivers
Strong UX instincts and genuine opinions about what makes a great customer experience
A data-driven, commercial mindset — programs that are too generous become liabilities, programs that are too stingy don't move customers, and you know how to find the right balance
Executive presence: you're comfortable presenting to C-level stakeholders, defending strategic tradeoffs, and working at that level of visibility
Experience working with marketing and CRM counterparts as close collaborators

A driver of how we use AI — both in the products we ship to customers and in how we do the job of product management. We're looking for someone who pushes the team further on this, not someone who keeps up

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with loyalty, rewards, or engagement programs is a plus but not a requirement

scope.surface: Loyalty & Referrals tribe — points-based loyalty program, referral program, and virality mechanics
primary_metrics_inferred: Loyalty program participation and reward redemption
Program economics (reward cost vs. commercial return)
Customer behavior tied to points and rewards
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, roadmap, and strategic direction for the loyalty program
Ship the milestones that move the business near-term, and balance them against bets that pay off over a longer horizon
Build the metric framework that connects customer behavior to program performance to commercial outcomes
Partner closely with marketing and CRM counterparts on program communications, reward economics, and commercial strategy
Manage external vendor relationships where they exist, evaluating partnerships continuously, and identifying where we build versus buy
Engage directly with C-level and senior leadership to communicate program direction, decisions, and results
Partner closely with the team leading referrals and virality — loyalty and referrals are closely related, and the strongest ideas span both

green_flags: Clear product ownership — loyalty program explicitly named as 'one of HelloFresh's most strategically important products'
Explicitly states loyalty experience is a plus but not a requirement, signaling openness in candidate pool
Flexible hybrid model described with transparency (2–3 days/week in office)
Active vacancy explicitly stated: 'This is a posting for an existing vacancy'
Broad scope with strategic visibility (C-level engagement, cross-tribe influence)
red_flags: Location/city not explicitly stated in the JD body — unclear which office this role is tied to
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements are not mentioned
Seniority requirements are vague ('significant experience') — no minimum years stated
AI integrated screening tools disclosed, which may affect application evaluation transparency
